name: xentral
description: Access Xentral ERP sales orders, products, and customers, and import new sales orders into your Xentral instance

envVars.name: XENTRAL_API_TOKEN
envVars.description: Xentral Personal Access Token (PAT), sent as Authorization: Bearer header

tools.name: List Sales Orders
tools.description: List sales orders in the Xentral instance with optional filtering and pagination

instanceHost.description: Xentral instance host, for example mycompany.xentral.biz

filter[0][key].description: Field to filter on, e.g. status, documentNumber, customerId, orderDate, externalOrderNumber

filter[0][op].description: Filter operator: equals, notEquals, in, notIn, greaterThan, lessThan, greaterThanOrEquals, lessThanOrEquals

filter[0][value].description: Filter value; statuses are created, released, canceled, completed; dates use ISO 8601 like 2026-06-01T00:00:00+02:00

tools.name: Get Sales Order
tools.description: Get a single sales order with its positions and financial details

instanceHost.description: Xentral instance host, for example mycompany.xentral.biz

tools.name: List Products
tools.description: List products in the Xentral instance (v2 API) with optional filtering and pagination

instanceHost.description: Xentral instance host, for example mycompany.xentral.biz

filter[0][key].description: Field to filter on, e.g. number, name, ean

filter[0][op].description: Filter operator: equals, notEquals, in, notIn, greaterThan, lessThan, greaterThanOrEquals, lessThanOrEquals

tools.name: List Customers
tools.description: List customers in the Xentral instance (v2 API) with optional filtering and pagination

instanceHost.description: Xentral instance host, for example mycompany.xentral.biz

filter[0][key].description: Field to filter on, e.g. name, customerNumber, email

filter[0][op].description: Filter operator: equals, notEquals, in, notIn, greaterThan, lessThan, greaterThanOrEquals, lessThanOrEquals

tools.name: Create Sales Order
tools.description: Import a new sales order into Xentral with customer, financials, delivery, and order positions

instanceHost.description: Xentral instance host, for example mycompany.xentral.biz

financials.description: Financial details: paymentMethod, currency, billingAddress, tax, paymentTerms

delivery.description: Delivery details such as shipping method and shipping address

positions.description: Order positions, each with product { id }, quantity, and price

externalOrderNumber.description: External order number from the originating shop or marketplace

customerOrderNumber.description: Order number assigned by the customer

tools.name: Get Product
tools.description: Get a single product with full details (v2 API) after finding it via List Products

instanceHost.description: Xentral instance host, for example mycompany.xentral.biz

tools.name: Get Customer
tools.description: Get a single customer record with full details including addresses (v2 API) — useful before creating a sales order

instanceHost.description: Xentral instance host, for example mycompany.xentral.biz

prompts.title: Review open sales orders
prompts.prompt: List my Xentral sales orders with status created or released and summarize them by customer and value.

prompts.title: Product lookup
prompts.prompt: Find a product in Xentral by name or number and show its details.

prompts.title: Import a sales order
prompts.prompt: Create a new sales order in Xentral for a customer and products I specify.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Xentral API setup

steps.title: Get a Xentral instance
steps.description: Sign up for a Xentral trial at https://xentral.com. Your instance gets its own host like mycompany.xentral.biz — all API calls go to that host.

steps.title: Create a Personal Access Token
steps.description: In your Xentral instance, create a Personal Access Token (PAT) via the API token settings (see https://help.xentral.com/hc/en-us/articles/22627542067740). Grant read scopes for sales orders, products, and customers, plus create scope for sales orders if you want to import orders.

steps.title: Store the token
steps.description: Add XENTRAL_API_TOKEN to your .env file. It is sent as an Authorization: Bearer header on every request.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Sales Orders tool with your instance host (e.g. mycompany.xentral.biz) as instanceHost.

notes: Every tool takes an instanceHost parameter — your instance subdomain such as mycompany.xentral.biz (custom domains are also supported)
PAT scopes are granted per resource group (CRM, products, accounting, ...) with separate read/create/update/delete permissions
Sales orders use the v1 API; products and customers use the newer v2 API endpoints
List responses wrap results in a data array with pagination info in extra
